name: Naida Caves
state: Dadra and Nagar Haveli and Daman and Diu
description: A surreal labyrinth of interconnected caves with natural sunlit shafts piercing through orange rock formations, creating a photographer's paradise just outside the city walls.
history: Believed to be formed when the Portuguese quarried building materials from these rocks to construct the Diu Fort. It also served as a hideout during 'Operation Vijay' in 1961.
famousFor: Natural rock formations, sunlit tunnels, and photography.
